title: Cephalomanes thysanostomum
taxonomy: Cephalomanes thysanostomum is classified under the phylum Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as ferns. This plant belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae, which is a family of filmy ferns. More specifically, Cephalomanes thysanostomum is placed in the genus Cephalomanes, which is a genus of filmy ferns native to Southeast Asia. The species name thysanostomum refers to the unique characteristics of its mouth-like structure. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Cephalomanes thysanostomum within the plant kingdom.

intro: Cephalomanes thysanostomum, also known as the forked lipwort, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cephalomanes thysanostomum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cephalomanes thysanostomum is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used to make infusions and tinctures to treat a range of health conditions.
